title: The One With Chandler And Monicas Weddin

jokes.text: Can this count as her 'something new'?
jokes.setup: Finding Monica's positive pregnancy test, traditional wedding saying about 'something old, something new'
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggesting pregnancy could count as the 'something new' tradition
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting wedding tradition with unexpected pregnancy, economical and well-structured

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica's pregnant!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so for the wedding... something old, something new, something borrowed, something blue... Does pregnancy count as the 'something new'?

jokes.text: It's turning into the worst wedding ever. The bride's pregnant, the groom's missing and I'm holding this.
jokes.setup: Wedding day disasters piling up
jokes.punchline: The absurd addition of holding a pregnancy test as the final indignity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure building to absurd climax with the pregnancy test

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And to top it all off, I'm holding a positive pregnancy test.

jokes.text: Just for the future. This is hardly a commercial for it.
jokes.setup: Asking about Monica's birth control method
jokes.punchline: Dryly noting that pregnancy is bad advertising for contraception
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with Phoebe's characteristic blunt honesty, very economical

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, pregnancy is really bad advertising for contraception.

jokes.text: It seemed like I called to chat. Pretty sure they both think I'm interested.
jokes.setup: Ross calling Chandler's parents about him being missing
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making it seem like he's romantically interested in both parents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross awkwardness, escalating an already uncomfortable situation

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hello, Mr. and Mrs. Bing? Hi, it's Ross Geller. I'm calling about Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, he's not in trouble. Well, actually, he's missing. But that's not why I'm calling.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just wanted to say... you both look absolutely stunning today. Really, truly.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And Nora, that perfume is intoxicating. And Victor, those slacks are very flattering.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know how Chandler came from such attractive parents. I mean, separately. Both of you. Individually attractive.

jokes.text: Give me two. Then why do you ask?
jokes.setup: Ross asking for one hour, then immediately asking for two
jokes.punchline: Ross's confused logic questioning his own question
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross overthinking moment, good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Can I ask you something?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Sure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And can I ask you two things?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You just asked me three things.

jokes.text: You're tired. You looked all night. And clearly, you suck at this.
jokes.setup: Joey offering to help find Chandler
jokes.punchline: Bluntly insulting Ross's search skills
jokes.explanation: Joey's characteristic blunt honesty delivered with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'll help you find Chandler. Ross, your search skills are terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I'm a paleontologist, not a detective.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you couldn't find your own ass with both hands and a map.

jokes.text: Maybe I should just forget about it. Become a lesbian or something.
jokes.setup: Rachel lamenting about never getting married while stalling Monica
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting becoming a lesbian as a solution
jokes.explanation: Somewhat dated humor relying on sexuality as punchline, but fits Rachel's dramatic character

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm never going to get married! I'm going to die alone!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, you're not going to die alone.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, maybe I should just become a lesbian.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Would that help?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know! It's worth a shot!

jokes.text: Maybe I'd feel better if I slept with Joey.
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing her dramatic spiral while stalling
jokes.punchline: Suggesting sleeping with Joey as therapy
jokes.explanation: Weak shock humor, doesn't build well from previous joke

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe this is happening. I'm a complete disaster.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're not a disaster, Rach.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, I am. I need to do something crazy. Something to shake things up.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know... sleep with Joey?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! I'm right here!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's therapeutic!

jokes.text: I'm having surgery.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to get out of work, claiming he can't work past 4
jokes.punchline: Suddenly claiming surgery after the wedding excuse fails
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Joey's panicked lying escalation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't work past 4. I have a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Director/Producer
dialogue.line: What thing?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Director/Producer
dialogue.line: Whose wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My... cousin's.

dialogue.speaker: Director/Producer
dialogue.line: Joey, we're shooting until 7.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, then I guess I'm gonna have to have emergency surgery that day.

jokes.text: Hair transplant. But you're not bald. It's not on my head.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming hair transplant surgery
jokes.punchline: Implying the transplant is for body hair in an inappropriate location
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Joey-style inappropriate oversharing, very economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm getting a hair transplant.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My head, where do you think?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, Joey. With you, it could be anywhere.

jokes.text: I'm wearing two belts.
jokes.setup: Richard the actor being drunk on set
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical statement about wearing two belts
jokes.explanation: Absurd drunk logic, very economical and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Richard
dialogue.line: I'm wearing two belts because one belt is a belt, but two belts? Two belts is a fashion statement.

jokes.text: How'd you guys find me? I knew I should've hid at the gym.
jokes.setup: Chandler being found at his office while hiding
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the gym as a better hiding spot (where he'd never go)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecation about his fitness habits, excellent character integration

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why would I hide at the gym? That's the one place I'd never voluntarily go.

jokes.text: And using the Internet to try to prove that I'm related to Monica.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining his panicking behavior
jokes.punchline: Desperately trying to find a reason not to marry Monica by proving they're related
jokes.explanation: Excellent panic logic, shows Chandler's neurotic desperation perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We could be related! We could be related!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, what if we're brother and sister?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, we're not related.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But what if we are? What if Mom and Dad—I mean, my mom and dad—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What if there's some crazy mix-up at the hospital and we're actually—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Chandler, no.

jokes.text: They yell, fight, and use the pool boy as a pawn in their sexual games.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why the Bings have bad marriages
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal marital problems to inappropriate sexual manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure from mundane to absurd, fits Chandler's family dysfunction

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, my parents had a very specific problem. My mother would... manipulate my father in ways that were... let's just say not appropriate for a PG-13 rating.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It started with the little things - nagging about his job, his friends. But then it evolved into... well, using physical intimacy as a weapon.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So basically, my dad never stood a chance. He was completely under her control. Which explains why I'm... the way I am.

jokes.text: Have you ever put on a black dress and asked me up to your hotel room?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to reassure Chandler he's not like his parents
jokes.punchline: Chandler referencing specific parental behavior as a test
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character history about Chandler's unconventional father

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You're nothing like your parents, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Because I just told Monica I'd do something, and now I'm in the bathroom hiding from her.

jokes.text: I don't race cars or make popcorn. None of my proceeds go to charity.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he's no Paul Newman
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly specific and absurd differences from Paul Newman
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from obvious to absurdly specific, very quotable and well-structured

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm no Paul Newman.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't have his eyes, I don't have his charm, I don't have his movies, and I definitely don't have his salad dressing empire.

jokes.text: But look, Chandler, right now no one has a lower opinion of you than I do.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to give Chandler a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Starting with an insult instead of encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross awkwardness, subverting expectation of supportive friend speech with brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, Chandler, listen to me. You're a mess.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, great pep talk so far.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, wait. You're a mess, but you're MY mess. And I believe in you.

jokes.text: Depends on what you mean by 'we.'
jokes.setup: Ross asking if taking a shower together isn't scary
jokes.punchline: Chandler's suspicious interpretation of 'we' taking a shower
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier homoerotic anxiety, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Taking a shower together isn't scary, right?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! 'We'? I think you mean 'you.'

jokes.text: Days and nights are hard, I get it.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving an overly dramatic speech about how hard nights are, then days
jokes.punchline: Monica cutting through the drama with blunt summary
jokes.explanation: Monica's no-nonsense personality cutting through Rachel's melodrama efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Nights are so hard. You're alone with your thoughts, and your thoughts are so mean to you. And then the days come, and you have to be around people, and people are even meaner than your thoughts.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So basically, you're miserable all the time.

jokes.text: Oh, good God, I've fallen down.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to stall Monica from getting ready
jokes.punchline: Dramatic fake fall with theatrical announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe over-the-top physical comedy with theatrical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! Oh no! I think I'm falling!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This is it. This is how it ends. Tell my story.

jokes.text: We can't find Chandler... His vest.
jokes.setup: Building up to tell Monica something terrible
jokes.punchline: Deflecting from missing groom to missing clothing item
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure, building tension then deflating with trivial concern

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Monica, I have something terrible to tell you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't find my shoes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your shoes? Phoebe, what about the groom?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, he's missing too, but I'm way more upset about the shoes.

jokes.text: For a minute, I was like, 'Oh, my God. The worst has happened.'
jokes.setup: Monica relieved it's just about a vest
jokes.punchline: Not knowing the actual worst has happened (groom is missing)
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience knows the truth Monica doesn't

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, it's just about a vest.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, Monica, there's something else you need to know.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The groom is missing.

jokes.text: Damn it.
jokes.setup: Ross telling Joey that Chandler showed up and everything's fine
jokes.punchline: Joey's frustrated reaction to missing the drama/chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction - disappointed to miss excitement rather than relieved, very economical

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do you mean everything's fine? I miss all the good stuff! I could've been here for Chandler showing up all dramatic!

jokes.text: Don't hang up on me. I'll marry you and me right now. I have the power.
jokes.setup: Joey panicking about missing the wedding while on movie set
jokes.punchline: Threatening to marry himself and the person on phone using his minister powers
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Joey's panic into absurd threat, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! You're telling me I'm missing the wedding? No, no, no, this can't happen!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm a minister! I got ordained online! I can marry myself!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: In fact, I'm marrying you too! Yeah, we're getting married right now over the phone!

jokes.text: They're my friends, Monica Stephanopolis. And Chandler Acidophilus.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get a Greek Orthodox priest to perform the wedding
jokes.punchline: Making up Greek-sounding names using yogurt bacteria
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Greek names with yogurt cultures, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Father Dimitrius, I really need you to perform my wedding ceremony.

dialogue.speaker: Father Dimitrius
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, but I can only marry Greek Orthodox couples.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But my fiancé is Greek! His name is... Lactobacillus Bulgaricus.

dialogue.speaker: Father Dimitrius
dialogue.line: That's not a Greek name.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Sure it is! And our children will be Streptococcus Thermophilus and little Acidophilus!

dialogue.speaker: Father Dimitrius
dialogue.line: Those are yogurt cultures.

jokes.text: You can't ask us, son. That's cheating.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about Chandler's whereabouts, claiming they're playing hide-and-seek
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dad seriously enforcing hide-and-seek rules
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with adult taking children's game seriously

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, where's Chandler? We're playing hide-and-seek.

dialogue.speaker: Charles
dialogue.line: He's hiding.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, can you tell him to come out?

dialogue.speaker: Charles
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. He's hiding, you have to find him. Those are the rules.

jokes.text: When we got married, I saw the groom in the wedding dress. That was after the wedding. It's not bad luck then. Honey, it isn't good luck.
jokes.setup: Discussion about wedding superstitions
jokes.punchline: Reference to Chandler's father's cross-dressing, with escalating clarification
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character trait with escalating punchline

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't see the groom before the wedding, it's bad luck.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh come on, that's just a superstition.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it's tradition. And you know what happened to Dad when he didn't follow traditions.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? My father wore a dress to the wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, your father wore a dress?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, but not because of the superstition. He just... does that.

jokes.text: Why would you play hide-and-seek with someone you know is a flight risk?
jokes.setup: Learning that Ross told people he and Chandler were playing hide-and-seek
jokes.punchline: Logical criticism of using that cover story with someone prone to running away
jokes.explanation: Excellent Monica logic and control-freak personality, sharp observation about poor planning

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You told people you were playing hide-and-seek? With Chandler? That's the worst cover story ever!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler runs away from everything!

jokes.text: You wouldn't happen to have a very big fork?
jokes.setup: Joey desperate to get off the movie set
jokes.punchline: Asking for a large fork (presumably to stab the drunk actor)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Joey considering violence, economical and surprising

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Can someone get me a large fork?

jokes.text: Is that my ass?
jokes.setup: Joey seeing himself on the movie screen
jokes.punchline: Questioning if he's seeing his own butt on camera
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag fitting Joey's vanity and actor concerns

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, is that... is that my butt?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it is!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How did they get that shot?

jokes.text: Hi-yah!
jokes.setup: Ross threatening to fight Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross's pathetic karate attempt
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross physical comedy callback, fits his nerdy tough-guy attempts

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You wanna piece of me? Huh? You wanna piece of this?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hi-YAH!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: What, you'll 'hi-yah'?
jokes.setup: Chandler mocking Ross's threat
jokes.punchline: Referencing Ross's pathetic karate sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm, immediate callback to Ross's embarrassing moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm so scared. What are you gonna do, hi-YAH me to death?

jokes.text: Cigarettes? Big picture, please.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning he went to buy cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically asking Chandler to focus on the important things
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Ross about priorities during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler, maybe you should focus on the important things in life.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know, like not buying cigarettes?

jokes.text: You obviously didn't see Chuckie III.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying anything that can fit in a baby shoe can't be scary
jokes.punchline: Reference to horror movie with scary doll
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference subverting Chandler's logic about small things

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Anything that can fit in a baby shoe can't be scary.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Have you seen Chucky?

jokes.text: I've never walked down the aisle knowing it can't end in divorce.
jokes.setup: Chandler's father walking down the aisle
jokes.punchline: Finding comfort in the permanence because he can't legally marry
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor about LGBTQ+ legal limitations, perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler Bing
dialogue.line: Well, at least he's found comfort in the permanence of it all. You know, because he can't legally marry.

jokes.text: Not that old crone. My mother.
jokes.setup: Wishing grandmother could see the wedding
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she means HER mother, not the other grandmother
jokes.explanation: Good family dysfunction humor, casual cruelty about in-law

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I wish my mother could see the wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your mother is here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I mean my *real* mother.

jokes.text: Not now.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if her dress is new during the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Monica's terse response during inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Monica's control-freak personality wanting perfect ceremony timing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, it is. Can we talk about this later? I'm getting married!

jokes.text: As are you.
jokes.setup: Being told someone is Greek Orthodox
jokes.punchline: Priest's confused assumption/response
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion humor, somewhat weak execution

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: Greek Orthodox? I thought you said Greek order fries!

jokes.text: You may be confused by this now, but you won't be Memorial Day weekend, 2002.
jokes.setup: Joey apologizing for being late to officiate
jokes.punchline: Mysterious reference to future date that makes no current sense
jokes.explanation: Very original meta humor, though impact may be limited without future context

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry I'm late, you guys. I was at an audition.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But hey, I'm here now, and I'm ready to officiate this thing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So, uh, we should probably get started before I have to be somewhere on February 14th, 2019.

jokes.text: Let's get started before the groom takes off again.
jokes.setup: Joey starting the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Chandler's flight risk publicly
jokes.explanation: Good callback acknowledgment, fits Joey's blunt honesty

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We are gathered here today to witness the union of Chandler and Monica. Chandler, I want you to know that I will always be here for you. Well, unless you try to run away. Then I'm tackling you.

jokes.text: As I've left my notes in my dressing room, we shall proceed to the vows.
jokes.setup: Joey officiating the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Admitting he forgot his minister script because he thinks of it as acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment mixing his actor life with minister duties

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I kind of treated it like a movie role, so I didn't really... I didn't write anything down.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You didn't write anything down?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, I figured I'd just wing it, you know, like I do in all my acting.

jokes.text: Unless you don't want to.
jokes.setup: Monica finishing her beautiful wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Sudden insecurity undercutting the romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis, undercutting romantic moment with typical insecurity, excellent structure

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you are my soulmate, my best friend, my everything. I promise to love you forever, to be there through every moment, to build a life with you that—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you DO think I'm prettier than Rachel, right?

jokes.text: Oh, wait. Do you take each other?
jokes.setup: Joey pronouncing them married
jokes.punchline: Realizing he forgot the actual 'I do' part
jokes.explanation: Good Joey incompetence moment, realistic minister mistake

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I now pronounce you husband and wife!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, did anyone say 'I do'?

jokes.text: Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Realizing they need rings
jokes.punchline: Joey's unprofessional reaction during ceremony
jokes.explanation: Simple Joey moment, economical but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Rings? Nobody said anything about rings!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I thought we were just gonna stand there and look pretty.

jokes.text: What baby? Our baby. We have a baby?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning he knows about the baby
jokes.punchline: Monica's confusion escalating to excitement about having a baby
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of misunderstanding, sets up reveal

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know about the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? How do you know about the baby?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm your husband, Mon. I was there.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! We're having a baby? We're having a baby!

jokes.text: Look at them. And they're gonna have a baby.
jokes.setup: Everyone confused about whose pregnancy test it was
jokes.punchline: Revealing Rachel is pregnant, not Monica
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation disguised as joke, good misdirection structure, high impact for story

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, whose test is it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not mine.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's... it's mine.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

insight: Wedding milestone episode underdelivers: 68.4 score, lowest density of season seven.
